codeReview.summary: The code change itself is correct and minimal: commit 34696d9 removes only the unused `strings` import from `test/thinking_conversion_test.go`, which matches the feature's stated repair path. However, the requested deliverable was a single small PR scoped to this one file, and the worker pushed the commit onto an existing F-1.5 PR that already contains many unrelated changes.

issues.description: F-0.1 required a dedicated small PR against `dev` limited to `test/thinking_conversion_test.go`, but commit 34696d9 was pushed onto existing PR #2864 (`feat(executor): header-based opt-out for OAuth cloaking levers`). That PR includes numerous unrelated file changes, so this feature was not delivered in the required isolated scope.
The implementation was appended to unrelated PR #2864 / branch `claude-cache-fix/F-1.5` instead of being delivered as its own one-file PR for F-0.1.

blockingIssues.description: `gh pr view 2864` shows title `feat(executor): header-based opt-out for OAuth cloaking levers`, head branch `claude-cache-fix/F-1.5`, and a file list spanning many unrelated files. This violates the feature requirement to ship the fix as a single small PR scoped to `test/thinking_conversion_test.go`.

sharedStateObservations.observation: The worker skipped the assigned `claude-cache-pr-worker` skill, used `mission-worker-base`, and then pushed the F-0.1 fix onto the existing F-1.5 branch/PR. The isolation rules in the feature skill appear necessary even for trivial fixes and should not be bypassed.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: worker-transcripts.jsonl skeleton for session 34d5bb7f-51f0-48e9-b479-dd034f08bfe5 shows `Skill {"skill":"mission-worker-base"}`; handoff.skillFeedback.followedProcedure=false; handoff.verification reports `git push ne0ark HEAD`; `gh pr view 2864` shows headRefName `claude-cache-fix/F-1.5` and title `feat(executor): header-based opt-out for OAuth cloaking levers`.

sharedStateObservations.observation: Mission docs point workers to repo-root `.factory/services.yaml` and `.factory/library/architecture.md`, but those files are absent in this workspace. That leaves the canonical validation commands undocumented at the advertised locations and likely contributed to ad hoc verification commands.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: Read attempts for `G:\Droid.Claude\CLIProxyAPI\router-dev\.factory\services.yaml` and `G:\Droid.Claude\CLIProxyAPI\router-dev\.factory\library\architecture.md` returned ENOENT; `LS G:\Droid.Claude\CLIProxyAPI\router-dev\.factory` shows only `validation/`; the transcript skeleton shows `go test ./test/... -run TestThinkingE2EMatrix_Suffix -count=1` instead of the mission baseline command described in mission AGENTS.md.

summary: Fail. The import removal itself is correct, but F-0.1 was not delivered as its own small PR: commit 34696d9 was appended to unrelated PR #2864 / branch `claude-cache-fix/F-1.5`, so the feature violates the required one-file, one-PR scope.
